name: Jiang Ziya
description: Jiang Ziya, also known as Jiang Shang or Lü Shang, is a legendary strategist, military theorist, and statesman in Chinese mythology and history. He is a pivotal figure in the Investiture of the Gods (Fengshen Yanyi), a classic work of Chinese literature. Jiang Ziya lived during the late Shang dynasty and early Zhou dynasty. He served as a key advisor to King Wen of Zhou and later his son, King Wu, in their rebellion against the tyrannical Shang ruler, King Zhou. Despite his advanced age and initial lack of recognition, Jiang Ziya's wisdom and strategic brilliance were instrumental in the Zhou dynasty's victory, ending the Shang dynasty and establishing a new era in Chinese history. He is revered as a master of military strategy, diplomacy, and Taoist practices. His teachings and strategies are still studied today, and he is considered a symbol of perseverance, wisdom, and the ability to overcome adversity. The Investiture of the Gods greatly embellished his life, attributing magical powers and divine assistance to his success. He is often depicted as an old man with a fishing rod, patiently waiting for the right opportunity. His influence extends beyond military matters, as he is also associated with divination and the creation of various Taoist rituals.
subtitle: Master Strategist and Divine Advisor
achievements: Served as chief advisor to King Wen and King Wu

keyMyths.title: Jiang Ziya's Fishing
keyMyths.description: Jiang Ziya famously fished with a straight hook, symbolizing his patience and waiting for the right opportunity. King Wen recognized his potential and recruited him as an advisor.

keyMyths.title: The Investiture of the Gods
keyMyths.description: After the Zhou victory, Jiang Ziya was tasked with bestowing divine titles upon the deceased heroes and villains of the war, thus solidifying the new cosmic order.

keyMyths.title: Defeating King Zhou
keyMyths.description: Jiang Ziya's strategic brilliance was crucial in planning the military campaigns against the Shang dynasty, ultimately leading to their defeat at the Battle of Muye.

sources.author: Sima Qian
sources.description: A comprehensive historical account providing information on the Zhou dynasty and Jiang Ziya's role.
sources.text: A comprehensive historical account providing information on the Zhou dynasty and Jiang Ziya's role.

sources.author: Xu Zhonglin
sources.description: A fictionalized account of the Shang-Zhou transition, depicting Jiang Ziya as a central figure with magical abilities.
